feedback.text: It would be nice if this Act also covered dark patterns used by companies selling digital products such as software or games. The dark patters in question are: 1. Stating the company is selling perpetual license to a software, but on a later date deciding to stop support for the software license activation servers, rendering the software unusable. 2. Selling software or video games as a license without clear "to the face" end date of the support for the software. This can result in users unknowingly buying a piece of media which can have its support cut within a few months, rendering it unusable.
